title: Commentary on Investments: As of May 31, 2026
content:
Portfolio Composition
As of May 31, 2026, total investments stood at $39,408,783, reflecting a year-over-year increase of 13.2%. Held-to-Maturity Debt Securities comprised the dominant portion of this portfolio, accounting for approximately 99.5% of the total value with a balance of $39,299,007.

Minor Holding Category
Other Investments represented the remaining fraction at $109,776, showing no change from the prior period.
The shift in total investments was driven by growth within the held-to-maturity category; however, without data on cash balances or other assets for this specific reporting window, any assertion regarding a reallocation of funds remains unsupported. The increase is therefore attributed solely to the reported movement in investment components.

Maturity Structure and Liquidity Implications
The maturity distribution chart illustrates that as of Q2 2026 (the most recent data point), assets are spread across multiple horizons, including buckets for maturities less than or equal to one year, greater than three years but not exceeding five years, and those extending beyond ten years. This diversified structure suggests the portfolio may support liquidity needs through scheduled cash flows from maturing securities while maintaining a presence in longer-term instruments.

Conclusion
The investment portfolio is characterized by an overwhelming concentration of held-to-maturity debt securities with a well-distributed maturity profile that provides predictable income and supports near-term flexibility without relying on active trading. The credit union continues to monitor the relationship between these scheduled maturities and its deposit liability structure.
